userName.description: Specify the new username for the user.

profileID.description:
Specify the ID of the new profile that you want to associate with the user account. The new profile settings are applied to the user account in the next backup cycle.Get the profile ID using the 'List all profiles' API.
 :fa-info-circle: If you are changing user's profile to a profile where the Privacy Settings conflicts with the Privacy Settings in the old profile, which may result in administrators getting an unintentional access to the user's data, inSync does not allow you to update the profile. In such a case, you must request the inSync user to allow the inSync administrators to access their backed up data (setting on inSync Client) and try again.

storageID.description:
Specify the ID of the new storage that you want to associate with the user account.Get the storage ID using the 'List all storages' API.
 After you change the user's storage:
 1)User data is backed up to the new storage.
 2)Snapshots on the old storage are retained and compacted following the retention policy.
 3) User can restore data from snapshots saved on old as well as the new storage.

delete.description: Deletes a user in the system. When you delete a user: 1. All the user data (restore points) is deleted. 2. If the user that is being deleted has shared data with guest users, this data is also deleted. 3. All devices of the user will get deleted, and all the active and preserved licenses that the user was consuming will be freed up. 4. You can roll back the deleted user from Rollback Actions until the rollback window lapses. 5. After the rollback window lapses, the user will be deleted permanently. For more information, see Rollback Actions.

post.description:
Preserves a user in Druva inSync.
When you preserve a user:
    1) Backups from the user's device are stopped and the user status changes to Preserved.
    2) inSync retains the user's data backups as long as the user is Preserved.
    3) If not activated again, the preserved user is later deleted from inSync after the duration specified in the  Auto-Delete Preserved User settings of the user's profile.

post.description:
Activates an already preserved user.
 When you activate a user:
    1) The user status would change from Preserved to Active.
    2) State of all devices assigned to this user would change from Disabled to Enabled.
    3) Backups from the user's device(s) would resume as per the configuration.
    4) Before activating a user, if a user has consumed, for example, one Endpoints preserved license, then post-activation one Endpoints preserved license is freed up and one Endpoints active license is consumed (the same rationale applies to all workloads).
    5) The license state of all the workloads associated with this user would change from Preserved to Active only if the user has Active licenses available for all the associated workloads.
 For example, consider this scenario wherein you are trying to activate a user who has consumed one Endpoints preserved license, one Microsoft 365 preserved license, and one Google Workspace preserved license, but the user has only one Microsoft 365 Active license and one Google Workspace Active license available, and Endpoints Active license is not available, then:
	a. the user activation would fail, as there is no active Endpoints license available.
	b. the user will remain in a preserved state, and the license state of Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, and Endpoints would continue to remain in the preserved state.

post.description:
Creates a data restore request and provides the option to scan the data for malware or viruses before restoring to the desired location on the target device. 
You can either restore the user's data to the same device from which it was backed up or to a different device registered with inSync. 
You can restore the data ONLY from the most recent snapshot to the target device. 
You can initiate multiple restore activities, which may be consolidated into a single activity based on the inSync Client version; appropriate status is displayed for each activity.

deviceID.description:
Specify the unique ID of the device in inSync from which you want to initiate the restore activity.
Get the ID of the device using the ‘List all devices’ API.

targetDeviceID.description:
Specify the target device ID to which you intend to restore the data.
The target device must belong to the same user.
Get the target device ID by using the ‘List all devices’ API.
Target device ID can be same in case you are restoring the data on the same device.

restoreLocation.description:
Specify the location to which you intend to restore the data on the selected device.
 Specify one of the following - 
1) Desktop - If you want to restore the data to the desktop on the target device. 
2) Original - If you want to restore data to the same location from which it was backed up. 
3) If you want to restore the data at a custom location, specify absolute path of the location. Example - /home/jeremy/statusReport

snapshotName.description:
To restore a curated snapshot, provide the curated snapshot name. Get the curated snapshot name using the 'List all the Curated Snapshots' API.
:fa-info-circle: Malicious file scan (enableAVscan parameter) is not applicable for curated snapshots.

enableAVScan.description:
If your organization has the 'Restore With Confidence' license, you can force scan the Snapshot for malware and viruses before restoring the data to the target device and location. Select True to enable Malicious File Scan for the Snapshot being restored.
:fa-info-circle: Malicious file scan is not applicable if you are restoring a Curated Snapshot.

get.description: Returns the list of all the events generated in Druva inSync. You can also get Events API response in CEF and Syslog formats. For more information, see Events API to export inSync events.

parameters.name: tracker
parameters.description:
Each Events API request returns a response that contains a maximum of 500 inSync events. If there are more than 500 events, the response also contains a Tracker, an identifier that contains inSync database reference and identifies the next set of unique events when a successive Events API call is made. Tracker is valid for the next 48 hours after a response is generated. You must include the Tracker in your subsequent request to fetch the next unique list of inSync events. Do not include the Tracker in the first Events API request. > ℹ️ **Info** > If you do not specify the Tracker in the successive request, inSync returns the same list of events in response that you received in the first Event API request.

**Ensure that you encode the tracker when you pass it as a query param**.

get.description:
Search for backed-up files using the SHA1 checksum value and view their details. 
While using this API, ensure the following: 
1. Make repeated API calls until the next page token is empty 
2. For each response, append the previous search results into the list until the next page token yields empty results 
3. This list is the final search result across the entire dataset 

 Note :
 SaaS Apps such as SharePoint Online and Shared Drive are not supported

parameters.name: sha1Checksum
parameters.description: Specify the SHA1 value of the file that you want to search. To know how to get SHA1 value of a file, see Search files using checksums.

parameters.name: minCreationTime
parameters.description: Filter to list the files which are created after a particular date and time. Format - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: maxCreationTime
parameters.description: Filter to list the files which are created before a particular date and time. Format - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: minModificationTime
parameters.description: Specify the minimum cut-off value of modification time to filter the list of files after a specific date and time. Format - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: maxModificationTime
parameters.description: Specify the maximum cut-off value of modification time to filter the list of files before a specific date and time. Format - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: fileType
parameters.description: Specify the file extensions by which you intend to filter the file search list. For e.g .txt

parameters.name: minFileSize
parameters.description: Filter to list the files based on their minimum file size (in bytes). All files with a size greater than mentioned value will be listed.

parameters.name: maxFileSize
parameters.description: Filter to list the files based on their maximum file size (in bytes). All files with a size less than mentioned value will be listed.

parameters.name: dataSource
parameters.description: Select a data source from which you want to list the files.

parameters.name: userID
parameters.description: Specify the User ID of the user for whom you want to filter and list the file results. Get the ID of a user using the ‘List all users’ API.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: The token to access the next page of results. Use the token value received in the previous response's parameter nextPageToken.

deviceID.description: The device ID of the device from which the file is backed up.

userID.description: The user ID of the user to which the file belongs.

storageID.description: The storage ID of the storage associated with the user.

sha1Checksum.description: The SHA1 value of the file.

fileSize.description: The size of the file in Bytes.

creationTime.description: The date on which the file was created (in UTC timezone).

modificationTime.description: The date on which the file was last updated (in UTC timezone).

folderPath.description: The absolute path of the file on the user device.

objectID.description: The unique identifier of the file to take further action based on the result.

nextPageToken.description: The token to access the next page of results. This parameter will be empty for the last page of results.

restoreLocation.description:
The location on the device to which the data was restored. 
The possibles locations are:
 1) Original: If the data was restored to the original location
 2) Desktop: If the data was restored to desktop
 3)Custom location: If data was restored to a custom location
 4) Unknown: If the restore was performed from a mobile device to a mobile device.

userID.description: The unique ID of the user who triggered the restore activity. Example: 12345

restoreID.description: The unique ID of the restore activity. This value is 'null' in case of restore was initiated from a mobile device. Example: 12345

startTime.description:
The start time of the restore activity. 
Example: 2013-04-17T09:12:36Z

endTime.description: The end time of the restore activity. Example: 2013-04-17T09:13:36Z. This value is empty if the restore activity is ongoing.

status.description:
Status of the restore activity. It is:
 1) Successful: If the restore activity was completed successfully.
 2) Ongoing: If the restore activity is ongoing. 
 3) Failed: If the restore activity was failed.
4) Cancelled: If the restore activity was cancelled manually.
 5) Completed with Errors: If some files were missed during the restore activity.

restoreSize.description:
The size of the data restored. Example: 285 MB 
 This value is 'Unknown' for restores that failed to start.

numberOfFilesRestored.description: The total number of files that were restored in the restore activity. Example: 456

numberOfFilesMissed.description:
The total number of files that were missed from the restore activity. Example: 12 
 This value is 'null' if the restore activity is ongoing.

restoreMode.description:
The persona who initiated the restore. Personas are: 
Self: inSync user himself 
Admin: Administrator.

systemSettingsRestore.description: This returns 'true' if system setting were restored else returns 'false'.

failureReason.description: This returns the failure reason if the status is 'failed' else returns 'empty.

Displays the location at which the data was restored. Example - Original.
Possible values are - 
1) Desktop - If the data was restored on the user desktop. 
2) Original - If the data was restored to the original location from where it was backed up. 
3) Absolute path of the location on the device if the data was restored at a custom location.

startDate.description: Displays the start time if the webdav access type option selected is 'Data within a Date Range'. Example- Apr 11 2021

endDate.description: Displays the end time if the webdav access type option selected is 'Data within a Date Range'. Example- Jun 12 2021

webDavAccessType.description: Details about the type of Webdav access. This field is case-sensitive. Example- 'All Collected Data' OR 'Last Collected Data' OR 'Data within a Date Range'

isADCEnabled.description: Indicates if Additional Data Collection feature is enabled for Legal Hold policy. Example-true/false

accessAdditionalData.description: Indicates if Additional Data access option is enabled for Legal Hold policy. Example-true/false

accessBackupData.description: Indicates if backup data access option is enabled for Legal Hold policy. Example-true/false

collectionFrequency.description: Provides details about additional data collection frequency. This field is case-sensitive. Example - One time/Recurring

deletedFilesOnly.description: Use 'true' to access only deleted files of users who belong to this Legal Hold policy. Else, use 'false'.

jobIds.description: List of Job IDs for which action is to be taken. Example-[115,114]

action.description: Specify the action that you want to take. Use any of the following:

message.example: Download Job has been Cancelled Successfully.

totalPages.description: Total number of pages for the API Response. Example - 10

pageNumber.description: Page Number for which API response is expected. Example - 1

pageSize.description: Page Size for API response. Example - 100

id.description: The unique ID of the AD/LDAP Connector in Druva Cloud.

name.description: The name of the AD/LDAP Connector configured in the Druva Console.

lastConnectedTime.description:
The date and time when the AD/LDAP Connector to Druva Cloud. 
:fa-info-circle: The date and time is displayed in Unix epoch time. Kindly convert as appropriate for further consumption.

lastStatusChange.description:
The date and time when the AD/LDAP Connector status changed from 'Connected' to 'Not Connected' or vice versa. 
:fa-info-circle: The date and time is displayed in Unix epoch time. Kindly convert as appropriate for further consumption.

status.description: The current status of the AD/LDAP Connector

total.description: The total number of AD/LDAP Connectors listed on the result page.

nextPageToken.description: The token to access the next page of results. This parameter will be empty for the last page of results.

adminAuditTrailRetentionPolicy.description:
The Audit Trail Retention settings in the duration of seconds. Example - 2592000 
 Here are the observed values and translation in days/months/year. 
- 2592000 → 30 days
- 7776000 → 3 months
- 15552000 → 6 months
- 31536000 → 1 year 
- -1 → Ever

id.description: Unique ID of the sensitive data type.

name.description: Name of the sensitive data type.

category.description: Category of the sensitive data type.

description.description: Description of the sensitive data type.

expressionType.description: Expression type of the Sensitive data type. Example - 'Keyword And', 'Keyword Or', 'RegEx'.

region.description: Region in which the data type is declared sensitive.

isCustom.description: Flag to determine if it is a custom data type.

query.description: Query run to identify the sensitive data type.

id.description: The unique ID of the policy template.

name.description: The name of the policy template.

createdBy.description: The unique ID of the administrator who created the policy template.

description.description: The description of the policy template.

includedMimetypes.description: The list of MIME type categories in the SDG policy template.

region.description: The region associated with the SDG policy template.

isCustom.description: Flag that determines whether SDG policy template is custom or not.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the policy template was last updated.

attachedDatatype.description: The unique IDs of the attached sensitive data types in the policy template.

id.description: The unique ID of the Sensitive Data Governance (SDG) policy.

name.description: The name of the SDG policy.

createdBy.description: The unique ID of the administrator who created the SDG policy.

description.description: The description of the SDG policy.

endUserAccess.description: Flag describing whether end user access is enabled to the policy.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the SDG policy was last updated.

attachedPolicyTemplate.description: The unique ID of the SDG policy template associated with the policy.

isRegex.description: Indicates if it is a whitelisted keyword or a regular expression.

whitelistKeywords.description: Displays a list of whitelisted keywords with either a keyword or is_regex properties.

isAutoResolveViolations.description: Flag describing whether auto resolution of violations is enabled or not.

daysToResolveNonCriticalViolations.description: The number of days after which non critical violations will be auto resolved.

daysToResolveCriticalViolations.description: The number of days after which critical violations will be auto resolved.

isArchiveScan.description: Flag describing whether archive files will be scanned for generating violations.

isAutoArchiveResolvedViolations.description: Flag describing whether resolved violations will be automatically archived or not.

daysToResolveArchivedViolations.description: The number of days after which archived violations will be automatically resolved.

pageToken.description: The token to access the next page of results.

id.description: The unique ID of the file violation.

resourceParentID.description: The unique User ID of the user for which the file violation is reported.

violatedPolicies.description: ID of the Sensitive Data Governance policy(s) based on which the violation was reported.

violatedDatatypes.description: List of ID(s) of the sensitive data type using which the violation was reported.

versionCount.description: The total number of versions of the file which is reported as a violation.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the file violation was last reported.

datatypeCount.description: The total count of the sensitive data type(s) using which the violation was reported.

version.description: The exact file version of the file for which the violation is reported.

resourceID.description: The unique ID of the device associated with the file for which violation was reported.

violationStatus.description: The current violation status of the file, that is whether it is Pending on User or Pending on Admin.

violatedFilePath.description: The absolute path of the file for which violation was reported.

mimetype.description: Mimetype of the file against which the violation was reported.

severity.description: The severity of the file violation, whether it critical or non-critical.

username.description: The name of the user for whom the violation is reported.

deviceName.description: The name of the device on which the violation is reported.

fileName.description: The name of the file in which the violation is reported.

violatedPoliciesName.description: The list of policy names for which violations are reported.

sensitiveDataFound.description: The type of sensitive data found.

comments.description: The additional comment (if any) is attached to the violation.

violationReportedOn.description: The date and time when the violation is reported.

resolvedOn.description: The date and time when the violation is resolved.

pageToken.description: The token to access the next page of results.

id.description: The unique ID of the email violation.

emailReceivedFrom.description: The sender of the email for which violation was reported.

mailTime.description: The date and time of the violated email.

emailSubject.description: The subject of the email for which the violation was reported.

resourceParentID.description: The unique ID of the user associated with the violated email.

violatedPolicies.description: List of ID(s) of the Sensitive Data Governance policy(s) using which the violation was reported.

violatedDatatypes.description: List of ID(s) of the sensitive data type(s) using which the violation was reported.

versionCount.description: The total version count of the email for which the violation was reported.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the email violation was last reported.

datatypeCount.description: The total count of the sensitive data type(s) using which the violation was reported.

version.description: The exact version of the email for which the violation was reported.

resourceID.description: The unique ID of the device associated with the email for which violation was reported.

violationStatus.description: The current violation status of the email, that is whether it is Pending on User or Pending on Admin.

severity.description: The severity of the email violation, whether it critical or non-critical.
